title: Incorrect Midpoint Calculation in Binary Search

statement.markdown: Implement a function `binary_search(nums, target)` that performs a binary search on a sorted array of integers. The function should return the index of the target element if it exists in the array; otherwise, return -1.
statement.inputFormat: A sorted array of integers `nums` and an integer target.
statement.outputFormat: The index of the target element if it exists in the array; otherwise, -1.
statement.notes: The input array `nums` is sorted in ascending order.

examples.explanation: The target element 5 is located at index 4 in the sorted array.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The binary search algorithm works by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half. The search should start at the middle element of the array and compare it with the target element.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a while loop to repeatedly calculate the midpoint and compare it with the target element.

functionSignature.java: public boolean binary_search(int[] nums, int target);
functionSignature.python: def binary_search(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> bool:
